claims.summary: Highly structured expression can remain semantically additive even when ordinary lexical residue is sparse.
claims.disposition: implement a structure-only semantic-review state rather than classifying all nonlexical residue as erased

claims.summary: Ordinary prose about residue or silt must not be removed merely because it resembles artifact language.
claims.disposition: verify that cleanup removes only exact known artifacts and retains ordinary lexical content

claims.summary: Boundary, contextual, and quoted marker cases need evidence distinct from a coarse residue count.
claims.disposition: verify marker occurrence, contextual boundary, and quoted-marker diagnostics while preserving output compatibility

claims.summary: Using structure-density evidence to change cleanup or provider behavior is a live semantic-policy change.
claims.disposition: require explicit Mike/operator Tier 5 approval before any behavioral use
